title: Assay policy-as-code (.assay-policy.json)
description: Team policy for Assay scans. Loaded from the scanning user's side (--policy flag or the working directory) — NEVER from inside the scanned target, so a malicious plugin cannot suppress its own findings.

suppress.description: Reviewed/accepted findings to silence before the verdict is computed.

id.description: Exact finding ID, or a trailing-'*' prefix glob (e.g. 'SCA-CVE-2021-*').

reason.description: Required justification for the audit trail.

expiry.description: Optional YYYY-MM-DD. After this date the suppression no longer applies and the finding resurfaces.

deny_categories.description: Fail the gate if any surviving finding is in one of these categories, regardless of severity.

allowlist.description: Target base names or full paths to skip scanning entirely (trusted first-party plugins).

fail_on.description: Default gate threshold when the CLI --fail-on flag is not explicitly set.
